title: Grass painting
content:
{{Top|leg}}
{{Quote|The artists would pick out their designs, using the flowers themselves as paint, the winds as their paintbrush, to create a natural spectacle to prove how humans and nature can work together.|[[Hari Seldona]]|<ref name="The Illustrated Star Wars Universe">''[[The Illustrated Star Wars Universe]]''</ref>}}
'''Grass paintings''' were a type of art used on the [[Planet/Legends|planet]] of [[Alderaan/Legends|Alderaan]]. Botanic artists would use the endless flat grasslands as their canvas. They used flowers as if paint and the wind as if a brush to "paint" a design.

[[Grass/Legends|Grass]] artists had to apply a five-[[Standard year/Legends|year]] license to create their art. The first would be the creation of the work while the others would be the painting becoming a collaboration the artist and the environment. The best artists were skilled in meteorology, though no one ever succeeded in predicting the weather, or growth-patterns.

One grass painter, [[Ob Khaddor]], was a troublemaker at [[University of Alderaan/Legends|Alderaan University]]. When he heard that [[Galactic Emperor/Legends|Emperor]] [[Palpatine/Legends|Palpatine]] was coming for a visit, Khaddar decided to create a painting "to honor the Emperor". He planted hundreds of thousands of seeds from various flowers to create a painting depicting the Emperor himself. When Palpatine came for a visit he rode on a [[Sail barge/Legends|sail barge]] to see the glorious portrait.

But when the Emperor looked, aided by his advisors and his [[Imperial Elite Guard|Imperial Guards]], [[Black lily|black lilies]] spread out across the Emperor's face, making his skin look old and crippled. The Emperor was outraged, and sent squads of [[Stormtrooper/Legends|stormtroopers]] to burn the painting to ashes.

Ob Khaddar left before the Emperor arrived. A [[Bounty/Legends|bounty]] was placed on him but no one had been able to track him down.
